info.name: AssignTrack API
info.description:
API test collection for AssignTrack (IFQ636 Assignment 2).

Covers every backend endpoint with happy-path and error-handling cases (401 / 403 / 404 / 400). Run top-to-bottom in the Collection Runner: the three Login requests capture JWT tokens into collection variables, and later requests chain the created subject/assignment/task/grade ids automatically.

The run is self-cleaning - the temporary subject, assignment and direct task it creates are deleted again at the end.

Set the `baseUrl` variable (or import an environment) to point at the live server (http://13.211.223.217/api) or a local backend (http://localhost:5001/api).

item.name: 1. Authentication & Authorization
item.description: Login for each role (tokens are captured into collection variables), plus auth-failure cases: bad password, duplicate registration, invalid role, missing token and role-based 403s.

exec: pm.test("API is reachable", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Subject routes are working"); });
pm.test("Returns a JWT token", function () { pm.expect(b).to.have.property("token"); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Invalid email or password"); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("User already exists"); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Invalid role"); });
pm.test("Returns a non-empty array of users", function () { var b = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(b).to.be.an("array"); pm.expect(b.length).to.be.above(0); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Admin access required"); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Not authorized, no token"); });

item.name: 2. Subjects (admin + Proxy access control)
item.description: Admin CRUD over subjects and the Proxy-guarded teacher/student assignment endpoints. A throwaway subject is created and deleted so the run is self-cleaning.

exec: pm.test("Subjects returned as an array", function () { pm.expect(subs).to.be.an("array"); });
var se = subs.find(function (s) { return s.name === "Software Engineering (Seed)"; });
pm.test("Seed subject is present", function () { pm.expect(se, "Software Engineering (Seed)").to.exist; });
pm.test("Returns the created subject id", function () { pm.expect(b).to.have.property("_id"); });

pm.test("Teacher attached to subject", function () { var ids = pm.response.json().teachers.map(function (t) { return t._id || t; }); pm.expect(ids).to.include(pm.collectionVariables.get("teacherId")); });

pm.test("Student enrolled in subject", function () { var ids = pm.response.json().students.map(function (s) { return s._id || s; }); pm.expect(ids).to.include(pm.collectionVariables.get("studentId")); });
pm.test("Description was updated", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().description).to.include("updated by the Postman"); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Admin access required"); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Subject not found"); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Subject deleted successfully"); });

item.name: 3. Assignment & Task lifecycle (teacher -> student)
item.description: End-to-end flow: a teacher authors an assignment that fans out to the enrolled student, the student updates/submits it, the teacher grades it, then the assignment is deleted to clean up.

item.name: Create assignment (teacher) — fans out to students

exec: pm.test("Returns the created assignment id", function () { pm.expect(b).to.have.property("_id"); });
pm.test("Fanned out to at least one student", function () { pm.expect(b.assignedTo).to.be.above(0); });

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Postman API Test Assignment",
  "description": "Created via Postman to exercise the assignment fan-out.",
  "deadline": "2027-06-30T23:59:59.000Z",
  "subject": "{{seedSubjectId}}",
  "assignmentType": "standard"
}

exec: pm.test("Includes the new assignment", function () { pm.expect(a.some(function (x) { return x._id === pm.collectionVariables.get("assignmentId"); })).to.be.true; });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Only teachers can create assignments"); });

item.name: Student fetches tasks — captures fanned-out task

exec: pm.test("Tasks returned as an array", function () { pm.expect(tasks).to.be.an("array"); });
pm.test("Task was created for the student", function () { pm.expect(t, "fanned-out task").to.exist; });
pm.test("Status moved to In Progress", function () { pm.expect(b.status).to.eql("In Progress"); });
pm.test("Priority set to High", function () { pm.expect(b.priority).to.eql("High"); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Status must be"); });
pm.test("Submitted task is now gradeable", function () { pm.expect(g.some(function (x) { return x._id === pm.collectionVariables.get("taskId"); })).to.be.true; });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Only teachers"); });
pm.test("Returns a grade with a label", function () { pm.expect(b).to.have.property("label"); });
pm.test("Includes the new grade", function () { pm.expect(gr.some(function (x) { return x._id === pm.collectionVariables.get("gradeId"); })).to.be.true; });
pm.test("Returns the created task id", function () { pm.expect(b).to.have.property("_id"); });

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Postman Direct Task",
  "description": "Temporary task created directly by Postman.",
  "deadline": "2027-06-30T23:59:59.000Z",
  "subject": "{{seedSubjectId}}",
  "status": "Not Started",
  "priority": "Medium"
}

exec: pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("managed by your teacher"); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Task deleted successfully"); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Assignment deleted"); });

item.name: 4. Notifications (Observer fan-out)
item.description: The student lists notifications raised by the assignment/grade fan-out and marks them read.

exec: pm.test("Notifications returned as an array", function () { pm.expect(n).to.be.an("array"); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Notification not found"); });

item.name: 5. Activity log
item.description: Central activity feed, plus an unauthenticated 401 case.

exec: pm.test("Returns an array of log entries", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json()).to.be.an("array"); });
pm.test("Returns the expected message", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.include("Not authorized, no token"); });
